other_observers: Nick A. Komar, Tom and Sherry Linnell, and a few others.; Yes
description: Smaller than a robin, larger than a house sparrow, otherwise typical passerine shape; body completely scarlet with jet black wings and tail; black eye; horn-colored bill. Bill was typical for a "Piranga" tanager, stout like a sparrow, but long like a warbler.Behaviors: Hopping around the cannopies of small trees while singing.Call: song a slow burry warble, like a "hoarse robin" similar to Western TanagerPlumage: adult alternate
